name: Aviation

spec.method_detail: Shake with ice. Double strain into chilled coupe.

history: Hugo Ensslin created the Aviation at the Hotel Wallick in NYC, publishing it in 'Recipes for Mixed Drinks' (1916). The crème de violette gives it a distinctive pale sky-blue-lavender color — hence 'aviation.' The violet liqueur was impossible to find in the US for decades, causing the recipe to float around without it. Crème Yvette's return in 2009 revived the original recipe.
